creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to Warner Losh from comment #17)

No clue what leads to what truss shows: not a
familiar context.

I'll note:

# acpidump -tv | grep OEMID
acpidump: loading RSD PTR from /dev/mem
acpidump: printing various SDT tables
	OEMID=QCOM, OEM Table ID=QCOMEDK2, OEM Revision=0x8280,
	OEMID=QCOM, OEM Table ID=QCOMEDK2, OEM Revision=0x8280,
	OEMID=QCOMM, OEM Table ID=SDM8280, OEM Revision=0x3,
	OEMID=MSFT, OEM Table ID=, OEM Revision=0x1,
	OEMID=QCOM, OEM Table ID=QCOMEDK2, OEM Revision=0x1,
	OEMID=QCOM, OEM Table ID=QCOMEDK2, OEM Revision=0x1,
	OEMID=QCOM, OEM Table ID=QCOMEDK2, OEM Revision=0x8280,
	OEMID=QCOM, OEM Table ID=QCOMEDK2, OEM Revision=0x8280,
	OEMID=QCOM, OEM Table ID=QCOMEDK2, OEM Revision=0x8280,
	OEMID=QCOM, OEM Table ID=QCOMEDK2, OEM Revision=0x8280,
	OEMID=QCOM, OEM Table ID=QCOMEDK2, OEM Revision=0x8280,
	OEMID=QCOM, OEM Table ID=QCOMEDK2, OEM Revision=0x8280,
	OEMID=QCOM, OEM Table ID=QCOMEDK2, OEM Revision=0x8280,
	OEMID=QCOM, OEM Table ID=QCOMEDK2, OEM Revision=0x8280,
	OEMID=QCOM, OEM Table ID=QCOMEDK2, OEM Revision=0x8280,
	OEMID=QCOM, OEM Table ID=QCOMEDK2, OEM Revision=0x8280,

in case the OEM involved tends to matter for the actual
interpretation of things.
